@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz models fitted with the M111 family

mainEntity.name: How much does an M111 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M111 typically costs £800–£1,800 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by Kompressor or naturally aspirated version. Fitting adds £390–£950 depending on labour rates and vehicle access.

mainEntity.name: Is the M111 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the M111 rates 4.0★ out of 5 based on specialist feedback and long‑term owner data [MB-INTERVIEW]. Its simple design and durable components make it one of the more dependable older Mercedes engines.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M111 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch is the most commonly reported issue, typically appearing from 80,000–120,000 miles. Head gasket leaks and oil leaks from the valve cover are also frequent on higher‑mileage units.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the M111?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M111 powers C‑Class (W202/W203), E‑Class (W210), SLK (R170), CLK (W208/W209), A‑Class (W168), V‑Class (W638), and some G‑Class and Sprinter variants — covering a broad range from the mid‑1990s to mid‑2000s.

mainEntity.name: How long does the M111 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With routine timing chain and gasket maintenance, the M111 routinely exceeds 200,000 miles. Many specialists report examples at 250,000+ with original internals [MB-INTERVIEW].

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M111 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes M111 is a 1.8–2.3L four-cylinder petrol — robust and simple, but timing chain and head gasket issues come with age. Backed by UK specialist consensus [MB-INTERVIEW].

verdictRating.confidence: 88% confidence — based on specialist feedback and long‑term owner data across multiple platforms.

rows.score: 20 (Most failures are cheap, well‑understood repairs (gaskets, sensors, chains))
20 (Parts cheap and widely available from multiple UK suppliers)
16 (Reconditioned cost is 30–44% of lowest host vehicle value (~£1,500))

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The M111 is a tough, simple petrol four — age‑related chain wear and head gasket leaks are common — buy on condition and service history, not just mileage.
verdictRating.bestFor: Classic Mercedes buyers wanting a dependable, easy‑to‑work‑on engine.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You're not prepared for routine gasket and chain maintenance on older cars.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M111 typically takes 6–10 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
